summary:
Monoclonal plasma cells downregulate MHC class I molecules, creating a "missing self" signal that triggers natural killer (NK) cell activation and destruction. This innate immune response enables early detection and elimination of abnormal plasma cells without requiring prior immunological priming.  

$\boxed{\text{C}}$

agent1 (Round 2 summarized): Monoclonal plasma cells downregulate MHC class I molecules, creating a "missing self" signal that triggers natural killer (NK) cell activation and destruction. This innate immune response enables early detection and elimination of abnormal plasma cells without requiring prior immunological priming.  

$\boxed{\text{C}}$. Answer choice: B
